strengths: Direct AI agent infrastructure experience at current role (MCP code generators, agentic workflow frameworks) — rare and explicitly required
Deep TypeScript/Node.js track record across multiple senior roles (re:cinq, Optilyz, RIDE Capital) with AWS CDK, MongoDB, and Docker/Kubernetes exposure
Strong testing culture evidenced by open-source test libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), TDD workshops, and explicit TDD/CI/CD practices at RIDE Capital
concerns: No mention of Nest.js anywhere in the resume — the primary backend framework for this role
React experience appears limited to one role (Optilyz); no Vitest or Playwright mentioned despite the job's testing stack emphasis
Kotlin is absent from the resume; Java is listed in skills but with no substantial project context, weakening the secondary stack coverage
response.justification: Bogdan is a strong match on the axes that are hardest to hire for: genuine AI agent infrastructure experience and deep TypeScript/Node.js seniority across diverse production contexts. The missing Nest.js exposure is the most notable gap, but given his demonstrated ability to pick up new frameworks quickly (DLang, Ember, Swift, Kotlin-adjacent Java) and his current full-stack role at re:cinq, this is a learnable delta rather than a structural mismatch. The thin React footprint and absent Vitest/Playwright mentions are worth probing in an interview but do not disqualify.
